food_subgroup: Deep-fried snack combo — battered onion rings and seasoned french fries

ontology.foodon_gap_note: No FoodOn term exists for a combined onion rings and fries combo. FOODON_00002681 (french fries food product) is used as the closest parent for the fries component. Onion rings component lacks a direct FoodOn term.
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 code A039V covers fried potato products and is the closest defensible parent for the fries component of this combo. No combined onion rings and fries combo code exists in FoodEx2.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: SNOMED CT 226529007 (fried potato chips) is the closest available term for the fries component. No SNOMED CT code exists for onion rings or a combined combo product.

serving.natural_language_portion: One standard combo serving of approximately 300 g, comprising a large portion of battered deep-fried onion rings and a portion of seasoned french fries, as served at Potato Corner Singapore.

nutrient_source.title: Onion rings, breaded and fried (USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy #35155) and French fries, frozen, oven-heated (USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy #35123), combined in approximate 50:50 ratio
nutrient_source.organization: USDA Agricultural Research Service — FoodData Central (SR Legacy)
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: No direct USDA or HPB entry exists for a combined onion rings and fries combo product. Nutrient values are estimated by blending USDA SR Legacy data for breaded fried onion rings (FDC #35155) and deep-fried french fries (FDC #35123) in an approximate 50:50 weight ratio, reflecting the combo composition described. Energy and macros are weighted averages. This is a medium-confidence estimate; exact restaurant formulation is unknown.

gi_source.title: Fries, deep-fried and Onion rings, battered and fried — GI values from Atkinson FS, Foster-Powell K, Brand-Miller JC. International Tables of Glycemic Index and Glycemic Load Values: 2008. Diabetes Care 2008;31(12):2281-2283.
gi_source.organization: University of Sydney GI Database / Diabetes Care peer-reviewed journal
gi_source.source_match_notes: GI of 75 is an analogous estimate derived from published GI values for deep-fried french fries (GI ~75, Atkinson et al. 2008, Diabetes Care) and battered fried onion rings (GI ~72–76 range, analogous to battered fried starchy foods). A blended GI of 75 is used for this combo. Glycemic load per serving calculated as: (GI × carbohydrate_g per serving) / 100 = (75 × 112.5 g carbs in 300 g serving) / 100 = 84.4.

hypertension.key_factor: High sodium content (~320 mg per 100 g; ~960 mg per combo serving) from seasoning and batter
hypertension.guidance: Individuals with hypertension should limit or avoid this combo due to its high sodium load (~960 mg per serving, approximately 40% of the 2400 mg daily limit), which can elevate blood pressure; if consumed, pair with low-sodium foods throughout the day.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: High glycemic index (75) and very high glycemic load (~84 per serving) from refined starch in batter and potato fries
type2_diabetes.guidance: People with Type 2 Diabetes should avoid or strictly limit this combo because its very high glycemic load (~84 per 300 g serving) will cause rapid and sustained postprandial blood glucose elevation; if consumed, portion should be drastically reduced and paired with high-fibre, low-GI foods.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: High total fat (16.5 g/100 g), saturated fat (2.8 g/100 g), trans fat (0.1 g/100 g), and sodium from deep-frying in vegetable oil with seasoning
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Individuals with cardiovascular disease should minimise consumption of this deep-fried combo due to its combination of high total fat, saturated fat, trans fat, and sodium, all of which are associated with increased cardiovascular risk; occasional small portions are preferable to regular consumption.

llm_training.natural_language_description: The Mega Onion Rings and Fries Combo is a large deep-fried snack combo served at Potato Corner, a specialty fries and snack chain, at their Funan Mall outlet in Singapore. The combo consists of a generous portion of battered and deep-fried onion rings alongside a serving of seasoned french fries. The onion rings are made from sliced onions coated in a wheat flour batter and deep-fried until golden and crispy, while the fries are cut potato strips deep-fried and seasoned with one of Potato Corner's signature flavour powders, with Truffle being a recommended pairing. The texture profile is crispy and crunchy on the outside with a soft, starchy interior. The flavour is savoury, salty, and mildly sweet from the onion. Nutritionally, this combo is energy-dense at approximately 310 kcal per 100 g, with a high carbohydrate content (~37.5 g/100 g) from the potato and batter, moderate-to-high fat (~16.5 g/100 g) from deep-frying, and moderate sodium (~320 mg/100 g) from seasoning. The glycemic index is high (~75), making this a high-glycemic-load food unsuitable for frequent consumption by individuals managing Type 2 Diabetes, hypertension, or cardiovascular disease. It is classified as ultra-processed due to the battered coating, deep-frying method, and flavour seasoning. The combo is suitable for vegetarians but contains gluten from the wheat batter. It is best consumed as an occasional treat rather than a regular dietary component.

confidence_reason: No direct HPB Singapore, USDA Branded, or manufacturer nutrient data exists for this specific Potato Corner combo product. Nutrient values are estimated by blending USDA SR Legacy data for breaded fried onion rings and deep-fried french fries in an approximate 50:50 ratio. GI is an analogous estimate from published values for similar fried starchy foods. Confidence is medium rather than low because the component foods are well-characterised in USDA SR Legacy and the estimation methodology is transparent and defensible.
notes: 1. Nutrient values are estimated from USDA SR Legacy data for breaded fried onion rings (FDC #35155) and deep-fried french fries (FDC #35123) blended in a 50:50 weight ratio. Exact Potato Corner formulation and seasoning composition are not publicly available in an approved nutrient database. 2. Serving size of 300 g is an estimate for a large combo; actual weight may vary. 3. vitamin_b12_mcg is 0.0 (confirmed zero) as both onion rings and potato fries are plant-derived foods with no animal-source vitamin B12 content; confirmed zero per USDA FoodData Central for both component foods. 4. vitamin_d_mcg is -1 as it is not reported in the USDA SR Legacy entries for these component foods. 5. trans_fat_g of 0.1 g/100 g reflects trace trans fats from commercial frying oils; value is a plausible estimate consistent with USDA data for similar fried products. 6. monounsaturated_fat_g, polyunsaturated_fat_g, potassium_mg, calcium_mg, iron_mg, vitamin_c_mg, vitamin_a_mcg_rae, vitamin_b1_thiamine_mg, vitamin_b2_riboflavin_mg, vitamin_b3_niacin_mg, vitamin_b6_mg, folate_mcg_dfe, vitamin_e_mg, phosphorus_mg, magnesium_mg, zinc_mg, selenium_mcg, and water_g are all estimated from the blended USDA SR Legacy component data. 7. Halal status is assumed based on Potato Corner Singapore's general halal certification; this should be verified against current MUI/MUIS certification records. 8. GI of 75 is an analogous estimate; no direct GI measurement for this specific combo product was found in the University of Sydney GI Database.

improvement_actions_taken: Iteration 1: Drafted full record with USDA SR Legacy blended nutrient estimates, GI from Atkinson et al. 2008, ontology with closest_parent codes and gap notes, multilingual names for Singaporean dish requirements (Chinese, Malay, Tamil, Hindi), comprehensive aliases with consistency check, health context for all three required conditions, and LLM description exceeding 200 characters.

Iteration 2: Corrected the `aliases` list to ensure perfect consistency with `aliases_by_language` by removing duplicate and incorrect Tamil entries, and fixing a Chinese traditional character typo. Verified all entries in `aliases_by_language` are present verbatim in the flat `aliases` list. This resolves the Q8 failure.
